channel.title: NPR Topics: World
channel.description: NPR world news, international art and culture, world business and financial markets, world economy, and global trends in health, science and technology. Subscribe to the World Story of the Day podcast and RSS feed.
channel.copyright: Copyright 2024 NPR - For Personal Use Only

item.title: Food assistance slashed for hundreds of thousands of Rohingya refugees trapped in Bangladesh camps
item.description: Hundreds of thousands of Rohingya refugees struggling to survive in Bangladesh's overcrowded camps will see their food assistance slashed starting on Wednesday, raising alarm throughout the increasingly desperate community.
item.content:encoded:
Hundreds of thousands of Rohingya refugees struggling to survive in Bangladesh's overcrowded camps will see their food assistance slashed starting on Wednesday, raising alarm throughout the increasingly desperate community.

(Image credit: Mahmud Hossain Opu)
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press

item.title: Kherson, Ukraine: A hometown changed (Pt. 1)
item.description: Four years of Russia's all-out war on Ukraine have transformed not only Ukrainian cities but also how modern warfare is waged, in the first of this two-part story from Kherson.
item.content:encoded: Four years of Russia's all-out war on Ukraine have transformed not only Ukrainian cities but also how modern warfare is waged, in the first of this two-part story from Kherson.
item.dc:creator: Joanna Kakissis

item.title: Iranians debate whether the war is worth it
item.description: As the war in Iran enters its second month, and President Trump signals an end to the war, many Iranians are urging the U.S and Israel to keep striking their country.
item.content:encoded:
As the war in Iran enters its second month, and President Trump signals an end to the war, many Iranians are urging the U.S and Israel to keep striking their country.

(Image credit: Atta Kenare)
item.dc:creator: Emily Feng

item.title: One of the first people known to change their gender was an ancient Egyptian pharaoh
item.description: NPR art director and illustrator Jackie Lay tells the story of Hatshepsut, who is widely regarded as one of the greatest pharaohs in Egypt's history — but whose legacy was erased for over 3,000 years.
item.content:encoded: NPR art director and illustrator Jackie Lay tells the story of Hatshepsut, who is widely regarded as one of the greatest pharaohs in Egypt's history — but whose legacy was erased for over 3,000 years.
item.dc:creator: Jackie Lay

item.title: 6 books named finalists for the 2026 International Booker Prize
item.description: The shortlisted titles include novels and novellas from authors and translators spanning four continents, with stories that range from Japanese-controlled 1930s Taiwan to the streets of Tehran in 1979.
item.content:encoded: The shortlisted titles include novels and novellas from authors and translators spanning four continents, with stories that range from Japanese-controlled 1930s Taiwan to the streets of Tehran in 1979.
item.dc:creator: Anastasia Tsioulcas

item.title: DHS resumes asylum decisions. And, Iran's strike injures over 12 U.S. personnel
item.description: The Department of Homeland Security has lifted its ban on reviewing asylum applications. And, NPR has confirmed that an Iranian strike injured over a dozen U.S. personnel.
item.content:encoded:
The Department of Homeland Security has lifted its ban on reviewing asylum applications. And, NPR has confirmed that an Iranian strike injured over a dozen U.S. personnel.

(Image credit: Herika Martinez)
item.dc:creator: Brittney Melton

item.title: Iran war delays delivery of medical goods to other parts of the world
item.description: The war in Iran has slowed down international shipping, much of which contains medical and humanitarian goods destined for Asia and Africa.
item.content:encoded: The war in Iran has slowed down international shipping, much of which contains medical and humanitarian goods destined for Asia and Africa.
item.dc:creator: Fatma Tanis

item.title: Trump hints at an end to military action in Iran, saying U.S. will leave in 2-3 weeks
item.description: Trump's remarks came in response to a question about rising gas prices. He said they would fall once the U.S. leaves Iran, claiming that would happen in "maybe two weeks, maybe three."
item.content:encoded:
Trump's remarks came in response to a question about rising gas prices. He said they would fall once the U.S. leaves Iran, claiming that would happen in "maybe two weeks, maybe three."

(Image credit: Leo Correa)
item.dc:creator: NPR Staff

item.title: Russia is building in parts of Ukraine. What does it mean for a peace deal?
item.description: Russia is heavily developing occupied territory in Ukraine, suggesting it has no plans to return the land as part of any peace deal. NPR's Leila Fadel speaks with Reuters reporter Mari Saito.
item.content:encoded: Russia is heavily developing occupied territory in Ukraine, suggesting it has no plans to return the land as part of any peace deal. NPR's Leila Fadel speaks with Reuters reporter Mari Saito.
item.dc:creator: Leila Fadel

item.title: Figure skating season ends with redemption and heartbreak. What do fans watch next?
item.description: Worlds marks the last competition of the 2025-2026 season. Skaters have some time to go on tour, rest up and learn new routines before the next season starts in July.
item.content:encoded:
Worlds marks the last competition of the 2025-2026 season. Skaters have some time to go on tour, rest up and learn new routines before the next season starts in July.

(Image credit: Michal Cizek)
item.dc:creator: Rachel Treisman
